name: Sanshoku Hanami Dango Japan
desc: Chewy rice dumplings in pink, white, and green. Traditionally enjoyed during cherry blossom viewing season in Japan.

name: Soft Serve Matcha Cream Japan
desc: Creamy green tea flavored soft serve. A popular street food dessert found in tourist spots like Kyoto in Japan.

name: Kakigori Shaved Ice Japan
desc: Finely shaved ice topped with sweet syrups and condensed milk. The ultimate refreshing summer dessert in Japan.

name: Strawberry Christmas Shortcake Japan
desc: Light sponge cake with whipped cream and fresh strawberries. The quintessential celebration cake in modern Japan.

name: Purin Custard Pudding Japan
desc: A firm caramel custard similar to flan. A beloved retro cafe (kissaten) staple found throughout Japan.

name: Soufflé Fluffy Pancakes Japan
desc: Extra thick, wobbly, and airy pancakes. A major viral food trend and dessert specialty in Japan.

name: Shiroi Koibito Butter Cookie Japan
desc: Delicate cat's tongue cookies with white chocolate filling. A famous regional souvenir dessert from Hokkaido, Japan.

name: Dorayaki Red Bean Pancake Japan
desc: Sweet red bean paste (anko) sandwiched between two honey-infused pancakes. Famous as the favorite snack of Doraemon in Japan.

name: Ichigo Daifuku Mochi Japan
desc: A whole fresh strawberry wrapped in sweet bean paste and soft mochi. A seasonal spring delicacy in Japan.

name: Anmitsu Dessert Bowl Japan
desc: A bowl of agar jelly, dango, fruit, and red bean paste served with black sugar syrup (kuromitsu) in Japan.

name: Taiyaki Fish Shaped Cake Japan
desc: A warm, waffle-like cake shaped like a sea bream, usually filled with chocolate, custard, or red bean in Japan.

name: Melonpan Sweet Bread Japan
desc: A sweet bun with a crisp, cookie-dough crust that resembles the skin of a melon. A bakery favorite in Japan.
